notes: PARTIAL coverage — the current recipe drives three turns with an Esc+Esc keystroke between turns 2 and 3, but in the recording the rewind did NOT actually fire (transcript shows a linear parentUuid chain across all three turns with no truncation or branch). What this recording DOES prove: irrlicht's transcript_tailer handles three consecutive turns on the same UUID cleanly, the daemon emits a textbook ready→working→ready arc per turn, and the session_id stays stable across Esc+Esc / Enter activity. What it does NOT prove: that the daemon observes a true rewind (transcript mutation backwards). Follow-up: investigate claudecode's /rewind picker UI more carefully — Esc+Esc may need different timing or may require navigating a checkpoint picker (Up/Enter) before the rewind takes effect. Update the recipe + this notes block + the matrix's irrlicht_observes verdict once a recording captures the true rewind path.

text: Session appears in ready (pre-session or UUID, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Second user prompt drives ready → working
Second turn completes back to ready
Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after Esc+Esc, the session should stay in ready (the rewind itself is not a turn)
Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — third prompt after rewind drives ready → working on the SAME session
Spec: third turn completes back to ready
